validation_results.name: terraform validate
validation_results.command: cd infra/terraform && terraform init -backend=false -input=false && terraform validate

gate_results.log_path: Independently re-run 2026-07-22 against current infra/terraform/ (terraform v1.15.4, init -backend=false): 'Success! The configuration is valid' (one pre-existing, unrelated warning about a redundant vercel_project ignore_changes entry, not a INFRA-TF-001 regression).

definition_of_done_items.criterion: terraform validate exits 0 in terraform.yml; railway + vercel modules match the pinned provider schemas
definition_of_done_items.evidence: terraform validate: Success (re-run 2026-07-22). infra/terraform/modules/railway/main.tf and modules/vercel/main.tf use railway_service (not the legacy railway_deployment), a default_environment object, and vercel_project_environment_variable / vercel_webhook resources consistent with the pinned provider versions in infra/terraform/providers.tf.

notes: RETROACTIVE ATTESTATION BACKFILL (ENG-OPS-002.R16, remediating GOV-A-001). This task shipped in Sprint 18 without a canonical attestation.json (only task-tracking.json existed). Original delivery evidence located via independent git archaeology (git log --follow on the actual module files, not just commit-message task-ID grep, which is why the earlier reconciliation pass under-attributed this task): PR #286 'feat(infra): terraform SSOT — HCP backend, schema fix, tfvars + monitoring (INFRA-TF-004)' (merged 2026-06-06, commit 906430d0ae75ef4256b8d7495d7083575dce4f4f) is where the railway/vercel provider-schema fix actually landed (bundled with the INFRA-TF-004 scaffold in the same PR); PR #284 'docs(infra): add ADR-064 terraform SSOT, INFRA-TF tasks + iac plan' (merged 2026-06-06, commit 4c3a3b205676cfa1d545a91d313b2b6d5efa95a1) is the preceding task-definition/ADR PR. Both PRs independently confirmed MERGED via `gh pr view`. The module files were further touched by PR #308 (supabase prod-import safety), #320 and #354 (later infra hardening) — normal iterative maintenance, not evidence of non-delivery. Re-verified 2026-07-22 by re-running `terraform validate` and `terraform fmt -check` against the current tree (both green) rather than trusting the historical claim alone. This attestation was authored after the fact by a documentation/provenance backfill agent, not by the original implementer running the /exec ceremony live — hence no spec_path/plan_path ceremony fields.
